title: Ludo Kressh
content:

{{Quote|I fight for the Sith Empire, Naga Sadow… you fight for yourself!|Ludo Kressh, to Naga Sadow|<ref name="funeral">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]]</ref>}}
'''Ludo Kressh''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] in the years leading up to and during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], and was also the father of [[Elcho Kressh|Kressh the Younger]]. Descended from a mixed ancestry of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and members of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]], Ludo Kressh rose to be a powerful [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]], all the while clashing with his greatest rival, fellow Sith Lord [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]]. In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]] [[Death/Legends|died]], and both Kressh and Sadow angled for the title. Kressh led the deceased Dark Lord's [[Funeral of Marka Ragnos|funeral procession]] but was interrupted by the late arrival of Naga Sadow. 

Sadow proclaimed that the title of Dark Lord of the Sith was rightfully his, and an angered Kressh attacked him; however, their duel was stopped by Ragnos' [[Force ghost/Legends|ghost]]. After the fight, two outsiders landed in the middle of the funeral: [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travelers [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]]. Kressh feared that the siblings were precursors to an invasion by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], while Sadow believed they were the key to expanding the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire's]] territory.

Kressh and his rival disagreed repeatedly over the matter of the Daragons. Sadow, seeking to make his dream of a war with the Republic a reality, staged a false Republic attack on the Empire. Kressh was convinced at first and advocated bolstering the Empire's defenses against an impending Republic invasion. However, the Sith Lord soon discovered Sadow's treachery and along with his [[Ludo Kressh's faction|allies]], [[Battle of Khar Delba|attacked]] [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|his fortress]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Khar Delba]], but was subsequently outwitted and defeated by Sadow. Kressh then faked his death by sending his empty [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] toward Sadow's citadel, where it was shot down and destroyed. 

Without a strong opponent, Sadow—now the new Dark Lord—launched the Great Hyperspace War against the Republic. However, the Dark Lord returned to the Sith Empire with a diminished [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] after suffering a defeat at the hands of the Republic. It was then that Kressh revealed himself, telling Sadow that he had indeed faked his death. [[First battle of Korriban|Battle ensued]] over Korriban, and Kressh at first seemed to have taken the upper hand against his weakened opponent. However, the ever-cunning Dark Lord ordered one of his doomed [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to make a suicide run on Kressh's flagship, and the collision killed Kressh.

==Biography==
===Early life===
Ludo Kressh was born in the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Space]] area of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and lived in the period of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Timeline of galactic history/Legends|history]] known as the [[Golden Age of the Sith]]. He was descended from a mixed ancestry consisting of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]] and [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]].<ref name="pawns" /> Those Dark Jedi had first come to Sith [[Realspace/Legends|Space]] after clashing with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] at large in the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]].<ref name="EGttF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>

After they were defeated, the [[Exiles]], as they were known, were exiled to the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], where they discovered the harsh [[Burial/Legends|tombworld]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].<ref name="EGttF" /> Some Dark Jedi, such as Kressh's ancestors, intermarried and bred with native Sith.<ref name="pawns" /> At some point, Kressh was discovered to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and was [[Sith training|trained]] in the dark side of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], eventually rising to be a powerful [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. He formed a rivalry with fellow Sith Lord [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], with whom he clashed many times over the years.<ref name="funeral" />

Ludo Kressh had a son,<ref name="Paragon" /> [[Elcho Kressh]],<ref name="knighterrant" /> whom the Sith Lord believed shared much of his own likeness. While he spent his days engaged in battle, the Sith Lord's nights were spent in his [[Ludo Kressh's palace|palace]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rhelg/Legends|Rhelg]] where he labored endlessly on the creation of a [[Sith sorcery/Legends|magical]] device in the form of a [[Gauntlet of Kressh the Younger|Gauntlet]] that would potentially shield his son from all harm. He also employed an entire family of Human [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] to tend to his personal needs.<ref name="Paragon" />

As his slaves embraced the Sith teachings, they also increased in favor with him; one such was a thirteen year-old Human girl named [[Seelah Korsin|Seelah]], whom Kressh allowed the privilege of [[Ludo Kressh's pedicure set|caring for]] his feet. Though they had won his approval for all intents and purposes, Ludo Kressh's slaves were not above his wrath; he frequently beat them while in the midst of drunken fits of rage. When Seelah failed to cure an infection which resulted from an injured ankle Kressh received in battle, the Sith Lord began executing her family, causing the Human to flee for her life.<ref name="Paragon" />

===Arrival of the Daragons===
{{Quote|They must be precursors to an invasion…we should kill them at once!|Ludo Kressh to Naga Sadow, after the arrival of the Daragons|<ref name="funeral" />}}
[[File:LudoVsSadow.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Sadow and Kressh duel for the mantle of Dark Lord.]]
In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]] [[Death/Legends|died]] after more than a century of iron-handed rule. Ragnos' [[Funeral of Marka Ragnos|funeral]] was held shortly after, with Kressh leading the funeral procession as they laid Ragnos to rest in a [[Tomb of Marka Ragnos|tomb]] in the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]]. Following the Sith traditions and rituals, Kressh carried Ragnos' victory helmet, which he placed on Ragnos' sarcophagus for him to wear during his battles in the afterlife. Naga Sadow arrived late to take what he felt was his rightful place in the funeral's procession as the tomb was being sealed, and a furious Kressh stated that Sadow's insolence had shamed the memory of Ragnos. Kressh believed that his rival's progressive views were a danger to the Sith's existence, while Sadow held that the title of Dark Lord of the Sith was rightfully his and that the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] had grown stagnant as a result of centuries of inactivity.<ref name="funeral" />

The argument escalated into a duel, with Kressh initially driving Sadow back. Sadow eventually gained the upper hand after managing to stall Kressh using [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]]. However, their battle was interrupted by the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of Marka Ragnos, who warned that the fate of the Sith Empire hung in the balance and advised the two to choose their battles wisely. Kressh offered to reconcile with Sadow after this encounter, but their discussion was cut short by the arrival of a foreign [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. The ship identified itself as the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]'', and its crew, [[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], proclaimed that they had come in peace from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The pair was promptly taken into custody by [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] guards, with Kressh telling Sadow that the two should be executed immediately. Sadow, however, believed that the two should be interrogated, thinking that they may have valuable information.<ref name="funeral" />

The Daragons were jailed, and a [[Sith Council|council]] of ten Sith Lords was called to the [[Sith Citadel (Ziost)|Sith Citadel]] on [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] to discuss the prisoners' fate. Kressh contended that the two were [[Spy/Legends|spies]] for the Republic and the precursor to an invasion, and that they should be executed. Naga Sadow and an older Sith Lord, [[Simus]], believed that the two had valuable information and should be kept alive. Although Kressh expressed that his route was safer for the general health of the Sith Empire, Sadow proclaimed that the Daragons provided a window for the Sith to expand their Empire and control the very galaxy itself. The meeting ended without a definite conclusion drawn, but Sadow had his own plans.<ref name="fabric" />

The Sith Lord, along with Massassi warriors, attacked the prisoners' guards and freed them from their jail cells. When the scene was discovered, Simus was found dead, with his head resting near a Republic [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. The council of Sith Lords met again on Ziost after Simus' death, and Kressh was irate; he believed his fears of a Republic attack had been confirmed, that Simus had been killed by the Republic, and that the Sith Empire should bolster its defenses. Sadow, however, proclaimed that the time had come to fight back and pronounced himself the new Dark Lord of the Sith. The other Sith Lords agreed that Sadow should be Dark Lord; a furious and shocked Kressh left, proclaiming that he would take his [[Ludo Kressh's faction|supporters]] and refuse to join in his enemy's crusade and that he would become the true Dark Lord.<ref name="fabric" />

===Battle of Khar Delba===
{{Quote|We must make this a quick and devastating strike.|Ludo Kressh|<ref name="pawns" />}}
[[File:BattleOfKharDelba3.jpg|thumb|right|275px|Ludo Kressh's forces attack the citadel on Khar Delba.]]
Ludo Kressh subsequently sent Massassi guards to the ''Starbreaker 12''{{'s}} impound dock on Ziost to guard it. However, his guards were attacked and defeated by Massassi loyal to Naga Sadow. In his chambers, Kressh was alerted to the attack on the ''Starbreaker 12'', and believing that it was being attacked by the Republic, gathered his forces and rushed to the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. However, by the time Kressh arrived at the dock, the ''Starbreaker 12'' had already been stolen by Sadow's forces. Kressh, observing the carnage in the hangar, found Naga Sadow's emblem amidst the Massassi corpses and realized that Sadow was responsible for the attack.<ref name="pawns" />

Moreover, Sadow had been the one who had killed Simus, shooting him with the blaster and planting it at the scene to make it appear like a Republic assault. Kressh returned to Rhelg, where he met with two of his supporters, [[Horak-mul]] and [[Dor Gal-ram]]. He told them about the attack on the hangar and the theft of ''Starbreaker 12'' and revealed to them Sadow's complicity in the attack. He then ordered Horak-mul and Gal-ram to gather their forces so they could make a surprise attack on Sadow, with Kressh vowing to stop his rival before it was too late. With the assault force prepared, Kressh commenced an [[Battle of Khar Delba|attack]] on Sadow's [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|fortress]] on the planet [[Khar Delba]], taking the firing controls on his own ship so as to land a few personal hits.<ref name="pawns" />

Commanding the attack, Kressh ordered his ships to attack and destroy Sadow. Horak-mul and Gal-ram were behind him fully, commenting that they had caught the Sith Lord with his guard down and that victory was inevitable. Sadow responded by ordering Massassi warriors to man the citadel's [[Autoturret/Legends|turrets]]. Kressh shrugged this off, believing that the turrets were Sadow's only defenses and that victory was assured. However, Sadow had deceived his enemy; he had hidden a secret [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of [[Warship/Legends|warships]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around Khar Delba's [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Khar Shian]]. Meanwhile, amidst the chaos of the battle, Naga Sadow allowed Jori Daragon to take control of the ''Starbreaker 12'' and flee the Sith Empire. Daragon was able to evade Kressh's fleet and disappeared into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="flight">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 5]]</ref>

After Daragon's escape, Kressh was caught off guard when Sadow ordered his secret fleet to attack. A taunting Sadow informed him that he had struck at the wrong fortress; all of Sadow's defenses had been at Khar Shian. Sadow then set the next part of his plan in motion by ordering the [[Sadow'een|Massassi crews]] of Horak-mul and Gal-ram's respective ships, who had secretly been loyal to him all along, to betray and attack their masters. The two Sith Lords were killed and their ships commandeered by Sadow's minions, who then attacked Kressh. The surprised Sith Lord ordered his intact ships to flee, thus signifying his defeat.<ref name="flight" />

===Faking his death===
{{Quote|You are the greatest threat the Sith Empire has ever known, Naga Sadow—and you must be stopped! You are ''exiled'' from the Empire, forever!|Ludo Kressh|<ref name="end" />}}
[[File:HappyLudo.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Ludo Kressh—the new Dark Lord of the Sith—informs Naga Sadow of his return.]]
After Kressh's retreat from Khar Delba, Naga Sadow began a vast militarization of the Sith in preparation for a war against the Galactic Republic. His victory over Kressh garnered him the support of the remaining Sith Lords, who pledged their fleets and [[Army/Legends|armies]] to his cause.<ref name="desperate">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 1]]</ref> However, Kressh had a plan;<ref name="end" /> while the other Sith Lords met with Sadow on Khar Delba, Kressh sent his<ref name="desperate" /> empty<ref name="end" /> [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] to the planet. Kressh simultaneously transmitted a message to Sadow and his followers, proclaiming that the Dark Lord was leading them on a path of destruction. A confident Sadow ordered Gav Daragon, whom he had kept with him to become his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], to destroy the ship, seemingly killing Kressh.<ref name="desperate" />

Little did they know, Kressh had actually faked his death, intending to claim the title of Dark Lord of the Sith, for it would let him regroup, rearm, and attack Sadow when he was fully ready.<ref name="end" /> Sadow soon launched his fleets, following a [[Homing beacon/Legends|beacon]] he had placed on the ''Starbreaker 12'' to find the Republic.<ref name="forces">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 2]]</ref> His forces, upon reaching their destination, began the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. The Dark Lord attacked the Republic on two fronts: [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]], and the governmental [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="first">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]]</ref> Ludo Kressh capitalized on Sadow's absence by gathering a fleet and declared himself Dark Lord of the Sith<ref name="end" />— he also forbade his son Elcho from participating in the conflict, and made his child wait out the conflict at a hidden location.<ref name="knighterrant" />

[[File:LudoDeath.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ludo Kressh's death.]]
The preliminary sorties of Sadow's war effort were initially successful,<ref name="first" /> but the Republic was eventually able to drive back and defeat the Sith.<ref name="dogs">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]]</ref> He returned to his region of space defeated, with his fleet a shadow of what it had been before the war. Ludo Kressh revealed himself at that moment, proclaiming to Naga Sadow that he was the true Dark Lord of the Sith. Feeling victorious, a joyous Kressh declared that Sadow was the greatest threat that the Sith Empire had ever known and that he must be stopped. The new Dark Lord therefore banished Sadow from the Sith Empire forever.<ref name="end" />

However, Sadow would not go down without a fight, and a [[First battle of Korriban|battle over Korriban]] quickly ensued. Sadow's already-diminished fleet found itself reduced even further during the conflagration, and Kressh appeared the victor; however, the always-cunning Sadow knew there were other ways to win a battle. Finding himself with no other options, Sadow ordered one of his already-damaged ships to make a suicide run to collide with Kressh's flagship. The new Dark Lord protested to Sadow to stop the ship, but it was too late. The vessel crashed into Kressh's flagship, killing him.<ref name="end" />

===Legacy===
After Kressh's defeat and death during the First Battle of Korriban, Sadow was [[Second battle of Korriban|attacked]] again by Republic forces that had followed him to the Sith Empire. Sadow was defeated in the battle, as his forces had already been greatly weakened after Kressh's assault in the first battle.<ref name="end" /> After his death, Ludo Kressh was buried in a [[Tomb of Ludo Kressh|tomb]] cut into a [[shyrack cave]] in the Valley of the Dark Lords. In [[3951 BBY]], over a thousand years after the Sith Lord's death, the tomb was visited by the exiled Jedi Knight [[Meetra Surik]]. While in the chamber, Surik had several visions, including some of former Dark Lord of the Sith [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and his former Sith apprentice, [[Darth Malak]].<ref name="kotor2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Ludo should be ''blindfolded'' so he can't see the changing universe around him.|Naga Sadow|<ref name="fabric" />}}
[[File:SternLudo.jpg|thumb|220px|Ludo Kressh.]]

Ludo Kressh was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] who had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]],<ref name="fabric" /> red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and light-red skin.<ref name="pawns" /> Unlike his rival and counterpart Naga Sadow, Ludo Kressh was a cautious man, one concerned with the safety and well-being of the Sith Empire.<ref name="EGttF" /> Kressh was content with the holdings of the Empire and feared that foraging into the wider galaxy and challenging the Republic would be disastrous for its health. Even when Sadow faked the Republic attack and theft of the ''Starbreaker 12'', Kressh was a staunch advocate for remaining in Sith Space and bolstering their defenses, unlike Sadow, who wanted the Empire to strike at the Republic.<ref name="fabric" />

Kressh despised Sadow—whom he considered his greatest contender for the crown of Dark Lord of the Sith—with a passion. He saw his rival's more progressive views as potentially harmful for the welfare of the Sith Empire.<ref name="funeral" /> Kressh believed that the Sith would be polluted by other species if the Empire moved past the [[Stygian Caldera]], but would remain untainted if they kept their own region of space.<ref name="Paragon" /> Kressh had no reservations about clashing with Sadow through both words and in battle,<ref name="funeral" /> but when the bold Sadow claimed the position of Dark Lord of the Sith, Kressh decided to break off the war of words and retreat to gather his followers.<ref name="fabric" />

While Ludo Kressh cared little for his slaves, the safety of his young son was of primary concern. His alcohol abuse gave way to violence, and incompetence on the part of his servants incited murderous rampages.<ref name="Paragon" /> Kressh was also a very cunning man; he tricked Sadow with his empty<ref name="end" /> flagship<ref name="desperate" /> and faked his death, then reemerged after the Sith defeat at the hands of the Republic in the Great Hyperspace War.<ref name="end" /> Despite his wiles, Kressh was still outwitted by his rival on multiple occasions. During the Battle of Khar Delba, Kressh believed that he had caught his enemy off guard with a surprise attack on his fortress; however, the fortress was a decoy, as Sadow's forces were massed at Khar Shian. After the secret fleet attacked, Kressh was outsmarted by Sadow, who had Horak-mul's and Dor Gal-ram's subordinates mutiny and betray their masters.<ref name="flight" />

Later, during the First battle of Korriban, Kressh was overjoyed at seemingly defeating the weakened Sith fleet; however, Sadow turned the tables by targeting him with a doomed ship on a suicide run. Upon noticing the ship headed in the direction of his flagship, Kressh quickly lost his poise and begged Sadow to call off the ship, but to no avail.<ref name="end" />

==Powers and abilities==
As a powerful Sith Lord, Kressh was trained and proficient in manipulating the dark side of the Force.<ref name="EGttF" /> Kressh possessed some knowledge of [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]]<ref name="dss">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref> and a working knowledge of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith sorcery]], the latter of which he used to construct a device that would protect his son from all harm.<ref name="Paragon" /> Kressh was capable of using the exotic [[Battlemind]] [[Force power/Legends|power]], and was also able to augment his speed, strength, and ferocity by amplifying his [[Force Enrage|rage through the Force]].<ref name="dss" />

Kressh proved to be an equal in combat with his rival Naga Sadow, with both gaining the upper hand at different points in their duel on Korriban and suffering wounds of equal proportion to their opponent as they clashed with blades [[Force lightning/Legends|wreathed in lightning]] and Force-thrown objects.<ref name="funeral" /> Kressh was trilingual, as he was able to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] as well as the [[Sith (language)|Sith]] and [[Massassi (language)|Massassi languages]].<ref name="dss" />

==Equipment==
In battle, Kressh utilized a [[Sith war sword]] that he infused with the power of the Force before a duel. He wore fancy clothing and jewelry.<ref name="funeral" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Exiled by his Dark Lord,|The [[Tedryn Holocron]]'s narration that first mentioned Ludo Kressh in [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]]|<ref name="Tales1">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]]</ref>}}
The Dark Lord of the Sith who exiled Naga Sadow was first mentioned in [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|the first issue]] of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]] ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith]]'', written by [[Tom Veitch]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]]<ref name="Tales1" /> and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="DH">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/93-518/Star-Wars-Tales-of-the-Jedi---Dark-Lords-of-the-Sith-1-of-6|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith #1 (of 6)}}</ref> on [[October 4]], [[1994]].<ref name="Tales 1 date">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54571/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_dark_lords_of_the_sith_1994_1|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith (1994) #1}}</ref> Ludo Kressh was then introduced as Naga Sadow's rival in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|second issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'' comic series, which was written by Anderson, pencilled by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]],<ref name="funeral" /> and published by Dark Horse<ref name="TotJ DH">{{DarkHorse|url=comics/95-271/star-wars-tales-of-the-jedi---the-golden-age-of-the-sith-2-of-5|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Golden Age of the Sith #2 (of 5)}}</ref> on [[November 13]], [[1996]].<ref name="TotJ Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54591/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_golden_age_of_the_sith_1996_2|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Golden Age of the Sith (1996) #2}}</ref> The [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|fifth issue]] of the comic series ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire]]'' revealed Ludo Kressh to be the Dark Lord who exiled Naga Sadow from the Sith Empire.<ref name="end" />

[[Tomb of Ludo Kressh|Kressh's tomb]] appears in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', where the [[Meetra Surik|Exile]], the main playable character, has the option to take [[Ludo Kressh's War Sword]].<ref name="kotor2" /> In [[2008]], Kressh received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="CSWE" /> and in [[2012]], his legacy was expanded in both the ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]]<ref name="TOR" /> and the ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="TORpedia">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

==Appearances==
{{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Ludo Kressh}}
